It is impossible to describe the pangs which were produced at this moment. The most powerful of modern pens would be helpless at the task. The people of Baltimore stood fearing that they would be swept off the surface of the earth by the terrible mass of water which would fall on their city. They expected to see the Bay of Chesapeake empty itself upon them. Then, besides, the city, even should the waters not come upon it, would be terribly shaken up by the shock which would be produced. The monuments would be destroyed; its best quarters swallowed up at the bottom of the abysses which would open through the surface of the ground. These fears ran through the different parts of the globe which were not scheduled for submersion by the upheaval of the oceans.

The gun fires with colossal force: "the projectile, under the impulsion of 6,000,000,000 litres of gas developed by the deflagration of the [[gun-cotton|pyroxyle]], rose into space" (RM-001-065). Michel notes they are "shut up in a bullet at the bottom of a cannon 900 feet long" over "400,000 lbs. of gun-cotton" (RM-001-056).

The Volga is the great river of European Russia and the first water barrier on Michael Strogoff's road east. Nijni-Novgorod, Lower Novgorod, situate at the junction of the Volga and the Oka, is the chief town in the district of the same name. (MS-006-001) It is here that the courier must quit the railway and trust to river and road.

Mongolia is divided from its southern neighbour by the ruin of the Great Wall. Of that frontier built between Mongolia and China there remain only blocks of granite and red quartzite, a terrace of bricks and a few old cannons. (ASC-024-004) In a fantastical redrawing of the globe, a whole section of Asia from the Black Sea eastward fell under one scheme. (TT-015-018)

Its position in Illinois makes it a recurring reference point across the voyages. In *Master of the World* the mysterious machine is reported "in Illinois in the neighborhood of Chicago" (MW-004-013), and elsewhere the city hosts such spectacles as a "fat-man show at Chicago" (GM-002-036), a token of its bustling civic life.
